commands.review_summary:
Adds a 2 s minimum heartbeat scheduler refire gap for past-due timers, scheduler regression tests, a Discord voice mock signature tweak, and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. with high confidence from source and field logs. Current main has the stale past-due zero-delay re-arm path, and the PR discussion includes Raspberry Pi ARM64 before/after logs; I did not run the physical ARM64 setup locally.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed; this active PR already contains the focused code and test change, so maintainers should review and validate the current head.

target.title: fix #79380: [Bug]: Gateway CPU spin / crash loop on Raspberry Pi 4 (ARM64) — regression from 4.23 to 4.25+
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes #79380

### Issue
[[Bug]: Gateway CPU spin / crash loop on Raspberry Pi 4 (ARM64) — regression from 4.23 to 4.25+](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/79380)

### Root Cause
The heartbeat scheduler's `scheduleNext` function uses `rawDelay = Math.max(0, nextDue - now)`. When all agents are deferred by cooldown or flood guards but their `nextDueMs` values are not advanced, `rawDelay` becomes 0. The `finally` block in `run()` re-invokes `scheduleNext` with delay 0, creating an infinite `setTimeout(0)` hot-loop that saturates the Node.js event loop and starves all I/O (Telegram polling, model requests, session locks).

### Changes
- **`src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts`**: Added `HEARTBEAT_MIN_REFIRE_GAP_MS` (2 s) floor when `rawDelay === 0` to break the tight `setTimeout(0)` loop. The guard is generous enough to never mask a legitimate fast-recurring schedule but always breaks an infinite re-trigger cycle.
- **`src/infra/heartbeat-runner.scheduler.test.ts`**: Two new tests — one verifying the 2 s floor when next due time is in the past, and one confirming natural delays are preserved when next due time is in the future.
- **`CHANGELOG.md`**: Single-line fix entry under `### Fixes`.

### Verification
- `pnpm check:changed` passed: typecheck ✓ lint ✓ format ✓
- Self-review: no additional changes needed
- Rebased onto latest `origin/main`, TypeScript check passed

### Real behavior proof

#### Behavior addressed
Gateway CPU spin / crash loop on Raspberry Pi 4 (ARM64) caused by an infinite `setTimeout(0)` loop in the heartbeat scheduler when all agents are deferred but their schedules are not advanced.

#### Real environment tested
Not tested on physical Raspberry Pi 4 ARM64 (no hardware access). Verified via unit tests using Vitest fake timers that the tight-loop condition is prevented.

#### Exact steps
1. Configured heartbeat runner with a mock `runOnce` that returns `{ status: "skipped", reason: "disabled" }`, simulating deferred agents.
2. Advanced fake timers past the first scheduled tick.
3. Asserted all `setTimeout` delays are ≥ 2000 ms (HEARTBEAT_MIN_REFIRE_GAP_MS).

#### Evidence after fix
- All `setTimeout` delays in the test are ≥ 2000 ms when `rawDelay === 0`.
- Normal scheduling with future due times uses natural delays (not capped to 2 s).

#### Observed result
- Test: "enforces a minimum refire gap when the next due time is in the past" — PASS
- Test: "does not add extra delay when the next due time is in the future" — PASS
- `pnpm check:changed` — PASS (typecheck, lint, format)

#### What was not tested
- Physical ARM64 hardware reproduction (requires Raspberry Pi 4).
- Long-running gateway soak under load with multiple deferred agents.
- Memory/CPU profiling on the target platform.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
